code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test16-APR-2021Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, high chloride, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity, glucosuria, and mild ketonuria. These findings suggest impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease, with evidence of glomerular protein loss and reduced concentrating ability. No signs of urinary tract infection are present. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
